Jericho decided that Israel would not be its master. It closed itself up, being strongly fortified both by construction and natural features. They were foolish in this way, and their hearts hardened to their destruction; the miserable situation of all who strengthen themselves against God Almighty. God decided that Israel would be its master, and that quickly. No warlike preparations were to be made. By the unusual method of besieging the city, the Lord honored the ark, as a symbol of his presence, and showed that all victories came from him. The faith and patience of the people were tested and increased.

Wherever the ark went, the people followed it. God’s ministers, by the trumpet of the everlasting gospel, which proclaims liberty and victory, must encourage followers of Christ in their spiritual warfare. As promised deliverances must be expected in God's way, so they must be expected in his time. At last the people were to shout; they did so, and the walls fell. This was a shout of faith; they believed the walls of Jericho would fall. It was a shout of prayer; they cried to Heaven for help, and help came.

Jericho was to be a solemn and awful sacrifice to God’s justice, upon those who had filled up the measure of their sins. So He appoints, from whom, as creatures, they received their lives, and to whom, as sinners, they had forfeited them. Rahab did not perish with those who did not believe. All her relatives were saved with her; thus faith in Christ brings salvation to the house. She, and they with her, were rescued as brands from the fire. With Rahab, or with the men of Jericho; our portion must be assigned, as we possess or disregard the sign of salvation; even faith in Christ, which works by love. Let us remember what depends upon our choice, and let us choose accordingly. God shows the weight of a Divine curse; where it rests there is no escaping from under it; for it brings ruin without remedy.
